faq.question: How much does window replacement cost in the Bay Area?
faq.answer: With the current promotion, quality vinyl replacement starts at $499-$549 per window installed (regular $832-$915), with fiberglass, clad-wood and architectural products higher. Window count, sizes, glass and access move the number, so quotes follow on-site measurements.

faq.question: How much does it cost to replace all windows in a house?
faq.answer: Whole-house pricing scales with count: industry data for 2026 shows 8-10 windows at roughly $6,400-$22,000 and 12-15 windows at $9,600-$33,000. Replacing everything at once is usually cheaper per window than phasing the work.

faq.question: Do I need a permit to replace windows in the Bay Area?
faq.answer: Most Bay Area cities require a permit: San Francisco requires one for every window replacement, and Oakland for any residential or commercial swap. Deluxe Windows confirms the rule for your address and files the paperwork.

faq.question: How long does a window replacement project take?
faq.answer: Measurement takes one visit, manufacturing typically four to eight weeks, and installation one to three working days for most homes. Larger or specialty orders, such as clad-wood or steel, can extend the manufacturing window.

faq.question: Should I choose retrofit or full-frame window replacement?
faq.answer: Retrofit installation keeps the existing frame and costs less, which suits sound stucco openings common in the Bay Area. Full-frame replacement removes everything to the studs and is the right call when frames show rot, leaks or previous poor installs.

faq.question: Which window brands does Deluxe Windows carry?
faq.answer: Deluxe Windows carries Milgard, Marvin, Andersen, Anlin, Simonton, JELD-WEN, Ply Gem, Alside, Western Window Systems, Italwindows and All Weather Architectural Aluminum, so budget and premium projects can be compared in one place.
